steps.title: Choose the origin idiom
steps.content: The passage says African languages contributed to the Puerto Rican dialect, then gives Yoruba pronunciation as an example. The phrase must show that certain vowel pronunciations can be linked back to Yoruba as their source.

steps.title: Reject incoherent verbs
steps.content: Choice A means made known publicly, which does not express linguistic origin. Choice C means yielded or given up, which is illogical for pronunciations. Choice D means presented or provided, which does not fit the idiom can be ___ to. The answer is B.
